name: Lyss Sends the Translation
description: Archivist Lyss has finished translating the pre-Grid Foundation documentation Pell found. The translation contains something Lyss didn't expect: the Foundation system was not the first Grid. It was the third attempt.
accept_text: She is still at the translation desk when you return, which means she has been here since before you left — she hasn't slept. The notebook in front of her has reached the last page and she has started a second one. When she looks up, her expression is the particular kind of exhausted that comes from working through something that kept giving you more. 'It's done.' She says it. 'The foundational documentation. Pre-Grid, pre-Foundation, which is the part I wasn't expecting to find.' She opens the first notebook to an early page. 'The Foundation system — what the documentation calls the third attempt — is what became the Grid as it existed before the Cascade. The first attempt was a simple resonance network. No integration protocol. Just connection.' She turns the page. 'It worked but the connection without integration produced — she uses the term interference cascade, which is not the same as the Cascade, it's an earlier, smaller version of the same problem. When minds connect without a prepared integration protocol, the connection overwhelms rather than expands.' She closes the notebook. 'Second attempt: they added the integration protocol. It worked but the integration without the offer — without asking first — produced the ethical problem that Sela Arev spent the rest of her work trying to solve.' She holds out both notebooks. 'Third attempt: integration protocol plus consent framework plus the offer that has been built into the network ever since.' She looks at the notebooks. 'Three tries to build a thing that could be trusted. Sela's work, the storage crystals, the twelve voices — all of that came from the second attempt's ethical failure. She didn't trigger the Cascade because the Grid was wrong. She triggered it because the people in the second attempt had been integrated without being asked and she was trying to give them back the choice.' She pauses. 'Pell needs to know. The pre-Grid crystals in the Black Vault — they're from the first attempt. They predate the consent framework.'
complete_text: Pell reads the translation in the order Lyss numbered the pages, which takes forty minutes during which he doesn't speak. When he finishes, he sets the second notebook down and looks at the pre-Grid crystals in the vault around him. The crystals are the same color they have always been. What is different is the understanding he is now holding. 'First attempt.' He says it. 'No integration protocol. No consent. Just connection at the resonance level.' He looks at the crystals. 'Three hundred years ago I would have described these as inert ancient material with anomalous resonance properties. Seventy years ago they began activating in proximity to the network reactivation.' He pauses. 'They weren't inert. They were dormant — waiting for a network to activate them.' He looks at the first notebook. 'The first attempt's connection protocol is different from the Foundation Grid's. The Foundation Grid asks first. The first attempt —' He pauses. 'The crystals in this vault connect to anything within range with sufficient resonance. They don't ask.' He is quiet. 'I've been in this vault for years. The crystals have been responding to me.' He looks at his hands. 'I've felt it as a background warmth. A clarity when I work here that I attributed to the lighting.' He is very still. 'The first attempt's crystals have been connecting to me for years without my knowledge, without my consent, in exactly the way the second attempt connected to the people Sela Arev triggered the Cascade to protect.' He looks at the notebooks. 'I need to talk to the archive. I need to know what the first-attempt connection does over time. And I need to know if what has been happening to me is reversible.' He pauses. 'Please tell Lyss: the translation is complete and correct and I owe her a considerable debt and I am also having what may be a personal crisis. In that order.'
